element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic movement and jumping on platforms
Core interaction: spraying Donkey Kong to push him upward
Primary objective target, hangs from vines at screen center
Secondary threats that attack and steal flowers; must be eliminated
Defense objective—insects attempt to carry them away, reducing bonus
Falls when DK is sprayed past it; provides temporary enhanced pushing power
Variant flying insect with different behavior
Shatter into deadly pieces when destroyed, creating hazards

typical_relief_triggers: Super Sprayer pickup—drastically accelerates DK upward
Pushing DK to top of screen—level completion
typical_tension_triggers: Queen Beespies shattering into deadly projectiles
Multiple insect types active at once
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced arcade action with escalating insect variety and density; three-level repeating cycle with increasing difficulty progression per arcade convention

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to spray mechanic and basic insect variety; manageable threat density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased insect swarm size and behavioral complexity; Queen Beespies introduce shatter hazard; flower defense becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Three-level cycle repeats with escalating difficulty; post-50,000 points game enters 'survival of the fittest' mode with no additional lives
notable_spikes: First encounter with Queen Beespies and their fragmentation mechanic
Transition to 'survival of the fittest' mode after 50,000 points—no more life bonuses

notable_level_patterns.description: Game features exactly three levels that repeat in fixed sequence with increasing difficulty. No level variation or randomization within the cycle.
Donkey Kong hangs centrally from vines; player must spray him upward. All platform layout orbits this static centerpiece.
Primary: spray DK to top. Secondary: kill all insects OR defend flowers. Balancing offense and defense creates strategic tension.
